turns.user_message: It started a few days ago when I was just sitting in my car in the driveway, completely fine one second and then suddenly my chest felt tight. I tried to shake it off and just walk inside, but my hands wouldn't stop trembling. I kept telling myself it was nothing, just a weird glitch in my system. Now I find myself checking my pulse every time I sit down, waiting for that weird heaviness to come back. It’s honestly just exhausting to feel so on edge over nothing at all.

That same heavy feeling from the driveway keeps creeping back whenever I try to settle into my usual routine. I catch myself holding my breath without realizing it, like I’m bracing for a wave that never actually crashes. My thoughts start moving a mile a minute, jumping from one small worry to the next until everything feels completely out of control. I’ve started pacing around the room just to burn off this restless energy that won’t quiet down. It’s frustrating because I know logically there’s no real danger, but my body just refuses to listen.

The driveway moment feels like it’s happening on repeat now, except the waves are hitting harder and leaving me completely drained. I can barely sit still for ten minutes before my heart starts pounding and my vision gets all fuzzy around the edges. Every tiny sound makes me jump, and I feel like I’m constantly waiting for the floor to drop out from under me. My whole body is just stuck in overdrive, turning ordinary quiet moments into this intense, suffocating pressure. I just want to feel steady again, but everything inside me is vibrating with this relentless, unshakable tension.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This just started recently.
